what_it_does: Defines a Colors canvas for browsing, editing, copying, generating, refining, locking, snapshotting, and previewing warm-shifted design-system color tokens, plus helper functions for derived token maps and preview values.
observations: Contains a fixed BASE_PALETTE grouped into Brand, Surfaces, Ink, and Status tokens.
Merges base tokens with extras and groups them by group for display.
Uses window.claude.complete to generate palette groups and refine individual swatches from prompt text.
Uses lockedTokens to include preservation context in the generation prompt and to render per-swatch lock controls.
Stores base-token edits in colorEdits and routes color changes through setColorEdit.
Renders a live preview with kit previews, buttons, cards, and section panel examples.
Supports a snapshot compare flow for current edits versus saved edits.
Exports Colors by assigning it to window.Colors.
Relies on globals such as React, CanvasHeader, ViShape, RefinePop, KitPreviews, window.claude, and window.dsaToast.
standouts: Generation prompts encode explicit ConceptV palette constraints and locked-token context.
The canvas combines token management with immediate visual previews across product surfaces and component examples.
Snapshot support makes palette changes inspectable side-by-side within the same canvas.
conventions: Uses global React destructuring rather than module imports.
Uses function components and local helper functions in one file.
Mixes design-system CSS classes with extensive inline style objects.
Treats colorEdits as the override layer for base tokens and some extra-token refinements.
Requests compact JSON from the AI path and includes a fallback extraction parse.
concerns: The removeExtra prop is accepted but not used.
The Check contrast button has no click handler in this file.
isExtra is computed for each swatch but not used.
refineSwatch creates an updated extras array but does not apply it; extra refinement is routed through setColorEdit by name.
Snapshot restore creates newEdits but does not use it.
The type="color" input expects browser-compatible hex values, while generation and refinement validation accepts 3 to 8 hex digits.
Clipboard writes are attempted without handling rejection or unavailable clipboard APIs.
notes: This file is the interactive color-token canvas, combining palette browsing, AI-assisted palette work, manual overrides, locking, and live preview.
questions: How should extra colors be updated or removed from the parent state?
Is Check contrast intended to open a tool, run validation, or remain a placeholder?
Should generated or refined colors be constrained to six-digit hex for the native color input?
What parent behavior is expected when setColorEdit receives undefined during snapshot restore?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose, a palette browser with real AI color generation, matches the implementation and is expanded with editing, locking, snapshots, and live previews.
apparent_themes: Palette governance through locks and snapshots
Local visual validation through representative UI examples
intention_signals: The file seems intended to let a designer safely explore and adjust a constrained brand palette while keeping changes visible, reversible, and compatible with existing token semantics.
novelty: The palette browser embeds AI generation and per-swatch refinement directly into the token canvas, with locked-token context and live product-surface previews.
contribution: Contributes a warm-brand color-token interaction layer: grouped palette browsing, prompt-based palette expansion, single-swatch refinement, lock-aware generation, copy/edit affordances, snapshot comparison, and representative UI previews that a fused system would preserve as color-system workflow behavior.
summary_for_embedding: Colors.jsx is a React color-system canvas that displays ConceptV palette tokens and extras, supports manual and AI-assisted color edits, lock-aware generation, copy actions, snapshots, and live component previews.
intention_for_embedding: This was for interactively extending and validating a constrained warm-ivory brand palette while preserving token context and showing edits in realistic UI surfaces.

symbols.does: Renders the full color canvas for grouped tokens, generation, swatch editing, locking, snapshots, and live previews.
symbols.description: Colors is the main component for this file. It derives edited base tokens, merges them with extras, manages generation and editing UI state, renders grouped swatches, and coordinates actions through parent-provided handlers such as addExtras, setColorEdit, setSnapshot, and toggleLock.

symbols.does: Requests an AI-generated palette group from the current prompt and stores a parsed proposal.
symbols.description: generate builds a ConceptV-specific prompt that includes brand color constraints and any locked tokens, then calls window.claude.complete. It parses the reply as JSON, filters usable color entries, limits the proposal to eight colors, and surfaces parse or request failures through toast messages.

symbols.does: Adds all proposed generated colors to extras and closes the generation panel.
symbols.description: adoptAll maps the current proposal colors into extra token objects with the proposal group, sends them through addExtras, clears proposal state, closes the generator UI, and shows an adoption toast. It is a commit action for the generated palette proposal.

symbols.does: Copies a hex value to the clipboard and shows a toast.
symbols.description: copy is a small utility for swatch hex interactions. It uses navigator.clipboard when available and reports the copied value through window.dsaToast.

symbols.does: Requests an AI revision for one swatch and applies the returned hex value as an edit.
symbols.description: refineSwatch sends the swatch name, current hex, role, and user instruction to window.claude.complete with a JSON-only response contract. It validates the returned hex, updates base tokens through setColorEdit, and routes non-base token refinements through setColorEdit by name as well.

symbols.does: Builds a token map by combining default color values with a flat edits object.
symbols.description: makeTokenMap returns the subset of color tokens needed by preview rendering, using edits when present and otherwise falling back to the built-in ConceptV defaults. It normalizes the flat edit layer into a named token object.

symbols.does: Builds the preview value object used by PreviewBlock from a flat edits object.
symbols.description: makePreviewValues extracts the specific colors used as previewCanvas, previewSurface, previewGold, previewBronze, previewInk, and previewGoldSoft. It mirrors the live preview defaults so snapshot previews can reuse the same PreviewBlock shape.

symbols.does: Renders a representative visual preview using supplied color values and token map.
symbols.description: PreviewBlock displays a labeled preview area with KitPreviews plus sample buttons, a card, and a section panel. It uses the passed preview colors and tokens to show how palette edits affect several product-surface and component examples.
